title: Check if Geometric
text: A geometric series has a constant ratio between consecutive terms. Here $$\frac{18}{12}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{3}{2}$$ but $$\frac{24}{18}$$ $$=$$ $$\frac{4}{3}$$ — the ratio is not constant. This is arithmetic (common difference $$d$$ $$=$$ 6).

title: Can an arithmetic series have an infinite sum?
text: Arithmetic series with a positive common difference grow without bound. The terms never shrink to zero.

title: Conclusion
text: Because this is an arithmetic series with growing terms, the infinite sum is not defined.
